channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Colombian Fans Rally in Vancouver Ahead of World Cup Match Against Switzerland
item.description: Colombian soccer fans made a dramatic entrance in Vancouver, Canada, ahead of their World Cup Round of 16 match against Switzerland. The enthusiastic supporters gathered outside the team's hotel, crea...

item.title: GOP Megadonor Ken Griffin Backs Marco Rubio Over JD Vance for 2028 Presidential Bid
item.description: Ken Griffin, CEO of Citadel LLC and a major GOP donor, revealed at the Allen & Company conference that he intends to support Secretary of State Marco Rubio over Vice President JD Vance if both run for...

item.title: Insurance Industry Faces Challenges in Responding to Potential Cyberattack Scenarios
item.description: A simulation exercise conducted by CyberAcuView and industry experts highlighted the vulnerabilities of the insurance sector in responding to large-scale cyberattacks. The scenario, based on the poten...

item.title: Ondo State Auditor-General Questions N6.7Billion OSOPADEC Contracts Over Missing Documents
item.description: The Ondo State Auditor-General has raised concerns over the lack of procurement documents for seven contracts worth N6.7 billion awarded by the OSOPADEC agency. Auditors couldn't verify if the contrac...

item.title: Ariana Grande impersonator shares heartfelt message from singer about self-acceptance in new documentary
item.description: Paige Niemann, an impersonator of Ariana Grande, has revealed in her documentary series 'Turning The Paige' that the pop star sent her a direct message in 2019 after seeing her online content. The mes...

item.title: Pakistan's Water Crisis: A Call for Internal Reform Over Blame
item.description: The article argues that Pakistan's water scarcity stems from chronic mismanagement rather than India's actions under the Indus Waters Treaty. Despite receiving 140 million acre-feet of water annually ...

item.title: Sonam Wangchuk Demands Parliament March to Address NEET Scandal and Student Suicides
item.description: Activist Sonam Wangchuk has called for a peaceful protest march from Delhi's Jantar Mantar to Parliament on July 20, the first day of the monsoon session, amid the ongoing CJP protest over the NEET pa...

item.title: Trump Questions Accusations Against Maine Senate Candidate Graham Platner
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump expressed skepticism about the sexual assault allegations against Maine Senate candidate Graham Platner during a press encounter at Joint Base Andrews. When asked about whe...

item.title: APC Reconciliation Committee Works to Resolve Internal Disputes in Kwara
item.description: Retired Col. AbdulWahab Ademola Lawal, chairman of the Kwara State APC Reconciliation Committee, has called on party members to embrace dialogue and reconciliation after disagreements from primary ele...

item.title: Marcos Directs AFP to Strengthen Defense in West Philippine Sea Amid Tensions with China
item.description: President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. has directed the Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) to prioritize external defense operations in the West Philippine Sea (WPS) amid ongoing security challenges, accor...

item.title: Funke Akindele's hustle: Juggling multiple jobs to survive at UNILAG
item.description: Funke Akindele, a popular Nollywood producer, shared her struggles as a student at the University of Lagos (UNILAG), revealing she had to take on various odd jobs to survive. She admitted to doing nai...

item.title: US Conducts Strikes Against Iranian Targets in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The United States has intensified military operations against Iranian forces in the Strait of Hormuz, aiming to disrupt their ability to threaten international shipping. US Central Command confirmed a...

item.title: Bishnoi Gang's Criminal Activities Pose National Security Risk in Canada
item.description: The article highlights escalating concerns over the Bishnoi gang's criminal activities in Canada, which have expanded through lax immigration laws. Experts warn that the group's actions—linked to kill...

item.title: Long-term Health Impacts and Costs for Retired Elite Athletes
item.description: The article explores the ongoing health challenges faced by retired elite athletes, using former All Black Sonny Bill Williams as a case study. Williams, preparing for his fourth neck surgery, highlig...

item.title: Quebec Court Rules Mandatory Minimum Sentence for Child Sex Abuse Material Unconstitutional Due to Defendant’s Mental Health
item.description: A Quebec court has ruled that the mandatory one-year minimum sentence for distributing child sexual abuse material is unconstitutional, citing the defendant's intellectual disability and mental health...

item.title: Ekurhuleni officials face arrest over corruption allegations
item.description: Former Ekurhuleni city manager Imogen Mashazi has been instructed to present herself to police as part of an investigation into corruption and malfeasance within the municipality. This follows the arr...

item.title: Three-year-old boy undergoes six surgeries after horrific zoo incident
item.description: A three-year-old child remains hospitalized following a traumatic incident at Johnsons of Old Hurst zoo in Huntingdon, England. The boy was allegedly thrown into a crocodile enclosure by an unknown ma...

item.title: Three men get life sentences for murdering 18-year-old during schoolyard robbery
item.description: Three men have been handed life imprisonment for the murder of Lerato Nchabeleng, an 18-year-old Grade 11 learner who was stabbed to death outside his school in Protea North, Soweto. The Protea region...

item.title: BBC 6 Music launches 'Indie Forever' stream celebrating UK indie music history
item.description: BBC Radio 6 Music has unveiled its new BBC Sounds extension 'Indie Forever', a 24-hour stream dedicated to indie pop, rock, and classics from the 1980s to the 2010s. Launching on July 22, the stream w...

item.title: South African court ruling challenges Canada's rhino horn import ban
item.description: A South African High Court has dismissed the government's appeal against the export of over 500 rhino horns, potentially testing the adequacy of Canada's ban on raw rhino horn imports. The case involv...
